task_completion.description: The Avare app is open and the flight plan creation interface is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Avare application UI is visible, specifically showing the 'Plan' or 'Route' screen where waypoints can be added.

task_completion.description: KRHV (Reid-Hillview Airport) is added as the departure waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The identifier 'KRHV' is listed as the first entry in the flight plan's waypoint list.

task_completion.description: SJC (San Jose VOR) is added as the second waypoint, ensuring it is the VOR station and not the airport.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The identifier 'SJC' is listed as the second entry in the flight plan. It must strictly be 'SJC' and NOT 'KSJC'. Look for VOR indicators if present, but the exact text 'SJC' is required.

task_completion.description: KSQL (San Carlos Airport) is added as the destination waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The identifier 'KSQL' is listed as the third/final entry in the flight plan's waypoint list.

task_completion.description: The flight plan is saved with the name 'VOR_PRACTICE'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, text input field, or a list of saved flight plans displaying the exact name 'VOR_PRACTICE'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Avare app's graphical user interface to create the flight plan.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard UI interactions like tapping search bars, typing in keyboards, and selecting items from dropdowns or lists within the app.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Avare application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual interface matches the Avare EFB app (showing aviation maps, Plan/Find/Map tabs), rather than a web browser, text editor, or generic database viewer.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows, adb shell commands, or script executions visible that directly inject the flight plan into the app's database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The flight plan must be saved exactly as 'VOR_PRACTICE'. The required waypoints in order are KRHV (Reid-Hillview Airport), SJC (San Jose VOR/DME), and KSQL (San Carlos Airport). It is critical to distinguish between 'SJC' (the VOR station) and 'KSJC' (the airport). The flight plan must use 'SJC'.
